briefs.title: BC Consumer Protection Rules Tighten: $5,000 Low-Value Claim Threshold and $50 Future Performance Contract Trigger
briefs.summary: Effective August 1, 2026, BC's amended consumer protection rules set a $5,000 threshold for low-value claims (blocking dispute resolution clauses) and a $50 cumulative price trigger for future performance contracts.
briefs.action_required: Review all consumer contracts and dispute resolution clauses to ensure compliance with the new thresholds by August 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Business Practices and Consumer Protection Amendment Act 2025 — key sections take effect
